verses.text: Le psalmiste prie le Seigneur de le sauver des méchants.
Le psalmiste prie le Seigneur de le sauver des méchants.
Aiguisent leur langue comme un serpent... un venin d'aspic : Ces créatures dépeignent les méchants comme des personnes corrompues (voir **Pr 23.32** ; **Es 14.29** ; **Rm 3.13** ).
Le psalmiste demande une protection divine (voir **Ps 121** ), priant pour que le Seigneur le sauve des nombreux complots des méchants.
Le psalmiste demande une protection divine (voir **Ps 121** ), priant pour que le Seigneur le sauve des nombreux complots des méchants.

140:6 I said unto the Lord, thou art my God,.... He said this to the Lord himself; claimed his covenant interest in him, and expressed it in the strength of faith: and this he did when in the midst of trouble and distress; in danger of falling into the hands of evil and violent men; they imagined mischief against him; were bent on his ruin, and laid nets, snares, gins, and traps for him; when he applied to his God, who only could deliver him;
L'expression « Éternel, Seigneur » provient de la combinaison du nom Yahweh avec le titre Adonaï (Maître). Voir aussi **68.21** ; **69.7** ; **71.16** ; **73.28** ; **109.21** ; **141.8** .

140:8 Grant not, O Lord, the desires of the wicked,.... Of Doeg, as the Targum, and of other wicked men, who were desirous both of taking him, and of taking away his life: but the desires of such men are under the restraints of the Lord; nor can they fulfil them unless they have leave from him, which is here deprecated. The psalmist entreats he might not be delivered up to their will, or they have their will of him; see Ps 27:12.
Le psalmiste prie pour que Dieu juge les méchants.

Le psalmiste souhaite que le feu descende sur ses ennemis et qu'ils soient jetés dans les abîmes pour qu'ils ne puissent jamais attaquer de nouveau. Ces images préfigurent l'enseignement du Nouveau Testament sur l'enfer ( **Mt 5.22** , **29-30 ** ; **10.28 ** ; **18.9 ** ; **Mc 9.43-47 ** ; **Lc 12.5 ** ; **Jc 3.6 ** ; **2P 2.4** ).
Le psalmiste prie pour que Dieu juge les méchants.

140:12 I know,.... Here is a double reading: the "Keri", or marginal reading, is, "thou knowest"; an appeal of the psalmist to God, who knew the thoughts of the wicked concerning him, and their devices against him; as Kimchi: but the Scripture reading is, I know; expressing his full persuasion and assurance that the Lord will maintain the cause of the afflicted, and the right of the poor; of his poor and afflicted people, that are afflicted within and without, by men and devils;

140:13 Surely the righteous shall give thanks unto thy name,.... The same with the poor and the afflicted; who, though traduced by men, and evil spoken of and ill used by them, are righteous in the sight of God; being justified by the righteousness of Christ, which is imputed to them, and received by faith, in consequence of which they live soberly and righteously: these the psalmist knew and was assured they would give thanks to the Lord, and praise his holy name, for the righteousness by which they are made righteous, and for every other blessing of grace and mercy of life;
